docs.title: flet-geolocator
docs.text:
Adds geolocation capabilities to your Flet apps.

Features include: - Get the last known location; - Get the current location of the device; - Get continuous location updates; - Check if location services are enabled on the device.

It is based on the geolocator Flutter package.

docs.title: Platform Support
docs.text:
This package supports the following platforms:

Platform Supported Windows ✅ macOS ✅ Linux ✅ iOS ✅ Android ✅ Web ✅

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
To install the flet-geolocator package and add it to your project dependencies:

uvpippoetry 
uv add flet-geolocator

pip install flet-geolocator  # (1)!
  1. After this, you will have to manually add this package to your requirements.txt or pyproject.toml.
poetry add flet-geolocator

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
main.py
import flet as ft

import flet_geolocator as ftg


async def main(page: ft.Page):
    page.scroll = ft.ScrollMode.ADAPTIVE
    page.appbar = ft.AppBar(title=ft.Text("Geolocator Tests"))

    def handle_position_change(e: ftg.GeolocatorPositionChangeEvent):
        page.add(ft.Text(f"New position: {e.position.latitude} {e.position.longitude}"))

    gl = ftg.Geolocator(
        configuration=ftg.GeolocatorConfiguration(
            accuracy=ftg.GeolocatorPositionAccuracy.LOW
        ),
        on_position_change=handle_position_change,
        on_error=lambda e: page.add(ft.Text(f"Error: {e.data}")),
    )
    page.services.append(gl)

    settings_dlg = lambda handler: ft.AlertDialog(
        adaptive=True,
        title="Opening Location Settings...",
        content=ft.Text(
            "You are about to be redirected to the location/app settings. "
            "Please locate this app and grant it location permissions."
        ),
        actions=[ft.TextButton("Take me there", on_click=handler)],
        actions_alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER,
    )

    def show_snackbar(message):
        page.show_dialog(ft.SnackBar(ft.Text(message)))

    async def handle_permission_request(e):
        p = await gl.request_permission_async(timeout=60)
        page.add(ft.Text(f"request_permission: {p}"))
        show_snackbar(f"Permission request sent: {p}")

    async def handle_get_permission_status(e):
        p = await gl.get_permission_status_async()
        show_snackbar(f"Permission status: {p}")

    async def handle_get_current_position(e):
        p = await gl.get_current_position_async()
        show_snackbar(f"Current position: ({p.latitude}, {p.longitude})")

    async def handle_get_last_known_position(e):
        p = await gl.get_last_known_position_async()
        show_snackbar(f"Last known position: ({p.latitude}, {p.longitude})")

    async def handle_location_service_enabled(e):
        p = await gl.is_location_service_enabled_async()
        show_snackbar(f"Location service enabled: {p}")

    async def handle_open_location_settings(e):
        p = await gl.open_location_settings_async()
        page.close(location_settings_dlg)
        if p is True:
            show_snackbar("Location settings opened successfully.")
        else:
            show_snackbar("Location settings could not be opened.")

    async def handle_open_app_settings(e):
        p = await gl.open_app_settings_async()
        page.close(app_settings_dlg)
        if p is True:
            show_snackbar("App settings opened successfully.")
        else:
            show_snackbar("App settings could not be opened.")

    location_settings_dlg = settings_dlg(handle_open_location_settings)
    app_settings_dlg = settings_dlg(handle_open_app_settings)

    page.add(
        ft.Row(
            wrap=True,
            controls=[
                ft.OutlinedButton(
                    "Request Permission",
                    on_click=handle_permission_request,
                ),
                ft.OutlinedButton(
                    "Get Permission Status",
                    on_click=handle_get_permission_status,
                ),
                ft.OutlinedButton(
                    "Get Current Position",
                    on_click=handle_get_current_position,
                ),
                ft.OutlinedButton(
                    "Get Last Known Position",
                    visible=False if page.web else True,
                    on_click=handle_get_last_known_position,
                ),
                ft.OutlinedButton(
                    "Is Location Service Enabled",
                    on_click=handle_location_service_enabled,
                ),
                ft.OutlinedButton(
                    "Open Location Settings",
                    visible=False if page.web else True,  # (1)!
                    on_click=lambda e: page.open(location_settings_dlg),
                ),
                ft.OutlinedButton(
                    "Open App Settings",
                    visible=False if page.web else True,  # (1)!
                    on_click=lambda e: page.open(app_settings_dlg),
                ),
            ],
        )
    )


ft.app(main)

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Deployed online documentation: https://flet-dev.github.io/flet-geolocator/
  • Geolocator control new methods: distance_between_async
  • Geolocator control new properties: position, configuration
  • New dataclasses:
    • GeolocatorConfiguration
    • GeolocatorWebConfiguration
    • GeolocatorIosConfiguration
    • GeolocatorAndroidConfiguration
    • ForegroundNotificationConfiguration

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • Refactored Geolocator control to use @ft.control dataclass-style definition and switched to Service control type

docs.title: Breaking Changes
docs.text:
• Geolocator must now be added to Page.services instead of Page.overlay.
  • Geolocator method get_current_position_async parameters changed:
    • removed accuracy
    • location_settings renamed to configuration (type changed)
    • wait_timeout renamed to timeout
  • In all Geolocator methods, parameter wait_timeout renamed to timeout.
  • Removed sync methods from Geolocator:
    • get_current_position → use get_current_position_async instead
    • get_last_known_position → use get_last_known_position_async instead
    • get_permission_status → use get_permission_status_async instead
    • request_permission → use request_permission_async instead
    • is_location_service_enabled → use is_location_service_enabled_async instead
    • open_app_settings → use open_app_settings_async instead
    • open_location_settings → use open_location_settings_async instead
  • Enum GeolocatorActivityType renamed to GeolocatorIosActivityType
Initial release.

docs.title: Geolocator
docs.text:
Bases: Service

A control that allows you to fetch GPS data from your device.

This control is non-visual and should be added to page.overlay list.

docs.title: configuration: GeolocatorConfiguration | None = None
docs.text: Some additional configuration.

docs.title: on_error: ft.OptionalControlEventHandler[Geolocator] = None
docs.text:
Fires when an error occurs.

The data property of the event handler argument contains information on the error.

docs.title: on_position_change: ft.OptionalEventHandler[GeolocatorPositionChangeEvent[Geolocator]] = None
docs.text:
Fires when the position of the device changes.

Event handler argument is of type GeolocatorPositionChangeEvent.

docs.title: position: GeolocatorPosition | None = field(default=None, init=False)
docs.text:
The current position of the device. (read-only)

Starts as None and will be updated when the position changes.

docs.title: distance_between_async(start_latitude: ft.Number, start_longitude: ft.Number, end_latitude: ft.Number, end_longitude: ft.Number, timeout: float = 10)
docs.text:
Calculates the distance between the supplied coordinates in meters.

The distance between the coordinates is calculated using the Haversine formula (see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haversine_formula).

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION start_latitude

The latitude of the starting point, in degrees.

TYPE: Number

start_longitude

The longitude of the starting point, in degrees.

TYPE: Number

end_latitude

The latitude of the ending point, in degrees.

TYPE: Number

end_longitude

The longitude of the ending point, in degrees.

TYPE: Number

timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float DEFAULT: 10

Returns: The distance between the coordinates in meters. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: get_current_position_async(configuration: GeolocatorConfiguration | None = None, timeout: float = 30) -> GeolocatorPosition
docs.text:
Gets the current position of the device with the desired accuracy and settings.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION configuration

Additional configuration for the location request. If not specified, then the Geolocator.configuration property is used.

TYPE: GeolocatorConfiguration | None DEFAULT: None

timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float DEFAULT: 30

Returns: The current position of the device as a GeolocatorPosition. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

Note

Depending on the availability of different location services, this can take several seconds. It is recommended to call the [get_last_known_position][flet_geolocator.geolocator.Geolocator.get_last_known_position] method first to receive a known/cached position and update it with the result of the [get_current_position][flet_geolocator.geolocator.Geolocator.get_current_position] method.

docs.title: get_last_known_position_async(timeout: float = 10) -> GeolocatorPosition
docs.text:
Gets the last known position stored on the user's device. The accuracy can be defined using the Geolocator.configuration property.

Note

This method is not supported on web plaform.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float DEFAULT: 10

Returns: True if the app's settings were opened successfully, False otherwise. Raises: AssertionError: If invoked on a web platform. TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: get_permission_status_async(timeout: float = 10) -> GeolocatorPermissionStatus
docs.text:
Gets which permission the app has been granted to access the device's location.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float DEFAULT: 10

Returns: The status of the permission. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: is_location_service_enabled_async(timeout: float = 10) -> bool
docs.text:
Checks if location service is enabled.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float DEFAULT: 10

Returns: True if location service is enabled, False otherwise. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: open_app_settings_async(timeout: float = 10) -> bool
docs.text:
Attempts to open the app's settings.

Note

This method is not supported on web plaform.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float DEFAULT: 10

Returns: True if the app's settings were opened successfully, False otherwise. Raises: AssertionError: If invoked on a web platform. TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: open_location_settings(timeout: float = 10)
docs.text:
Attempts to open the device's location settings.

Note

This method is not supported on web plaform.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float DEFAULT: 10

Returns: True if the device's settings were opened successfully, False otherwise. Raises: AssertionError: If invoked on a web platform. TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: open_location_settings_async(timeout: float = 10)
docs.text:
Attempts to open the device's location settings.

Note

This method is not supported on web plaform.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float DEFAULT: 10

Returns: True if the device's settings were opened successfully, False otherwise. Raises: AssertionError: If invoked on a web platform. TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: request_permission_async(timeout: int = 60) -> GeolocatorPermissionStatus
docs.text:
Requests the device for access to the device's location.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: int DEFAULT: 60

Returns: The status of the permission request. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: notification_channel_name: str = 'Background Location'
docs.text:
The user visible name of the notification channel.

The notification channel name will be displayed in the system settings. The maximum recommended length is 40 characters, the name might be truncated if it is to long. Default value: "Background Location".

docs.title: notification_enable_wake_lock: bool = False
docs.text:
When enabled, a Wakelock is acquired when background execution is started.

If this is false then the system can still sleep and all location events will be received at once when the system wakes up again.

Wake lock permissions should be obtained first by using a permissions library.

docs.title: notification_enable_wifi_lock: bool = False
docs.text:
When enabled, a WifiLock is acquired when background execution is started. This allows the application to keep the Wi-Fi radio awake, even when the user has not used the device in a while (e.g. for background network communications).

Wifi lock permissions should be obtained first by using a permissions library.

docs.title: notification_set_ongoing: bool = False
docs.text: When enabled, the displayed notification is persistent and the user cannot dismiss it.

docs.title: notification_text: str
docs.text: The body used for the foreground service notification.

docs.title: notification_title: str
docs.text: The title used for the foreground service notification.

docs.title: GeolocatorAndroidConfiguration
docs.text:
Bases: GeolocatorConfiguration

Android specific settings.

docs.title: accuracy: GeolocatorPositionAccuracy = GeolocatorPositionAccuracy.BEST
docs.text: Defines the desired accuracy that should be used to determine the location data.

docs.title: distance_filter: int = 0
docs.text:
The minimum distance (measured in meters) a device must move horizontally before an update event is generated.

Set to 0 when you want to be notified of all movements.

docs.title: interval_duration: ft.DurationValue = field(default_factory=lambda: ft.Duration(milliseconds=5000))
docs.text: The desired interval for active location updates.

docs.title: time_limit: ft.DurationValue = None
docs.text:
Specifies a timeout interval.

For no time limit, set to None.

docs.title: use_msl_altitude: bool = False
docs.text:
Whether altitude should be calculated as MSL (EGM2008) from NMEA messages and reported as the altitude instead of using the geoidal height (WSG84). Setting this property true will help to align Android altitude to that of iOS which uses MSL.

If the NMEA message is empty then the altitude reported will still be the standard WSG84 altitude from the GPS receiver.

MSL Altitude is only available starting from Android N and not all devices support NMEA message returning $GPGGA sequences.

This property only works with position stream updates and has no effect when getting the current position or last known position.

docs.title: GeolocatorPosition
docs.text: Detailed location information.

docs.title: accuracy: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The estimated horizontal accuracy of the position in meters.

The accuracy is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: altitude: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The altitude of the device in meters.

The altitude is not available on all devices. In these cases the returned value is 0.0.

docs.title: altitude_accuracy: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The estimated vertical accuracy of the position in meters.

The accuracy is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: floor: int | None = None
docs.text:
The floor specifies the floor of the building on which the device is located.

The floor property is only available on iOS and only when the information is available. In all other cases this value will be None.

docs.title: heading: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The heading in which the device is traveling in degrees.

The heading is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: heading_accuracy: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The estimated heading accuracy of the position in degrees.

The heading accuracy is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: latitude: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text: The latitude of this position in degrees normalized to the interval -90.0 to +90.0 (both inclusive).

docs.title: longitude: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text: The longitude of the position in degrees normalized to the interval -180 (exclusive) to +180 (inclusive).

docs.title: mocked: bool | None = None
docs.text:
Will be True on Android (starting from API level 18) when the location came from the mocked provider.

On iOS this value will always be False.

docs.title: speed: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The speed at which the device is traveling in meters per second over ground.

The speed is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: speed_accuracy: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The estimated speed accuracy of this position, in meters per second.

The speed accuracy is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: timestamp: datetime.datetime = None
docs.text: The time at which this position was determined.

docs.title: GeolocatorIosActivityType
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Represents the possible iOS activity types.

docs.title: AIRBORNE = 'airborne'
docs.text: The location manager is being used specifically during airborne activities.

docs.title: AUTOMOTIVE_NAVIGATION = 'automotiveNavigation'
docs.text: The location manager is being used specifically during vehicular navigation to track location changes to the automobile.

docs.title: FITNESS = 'fitness'
docs.text: The location manager is being used to track fitness activities such as walking, running, cycling, and so on.

docs.title: OTHER = 'other'
docs.text: The location manager is being used for an unknown activity.

docs.title: OTHER_NAVIGATION = 'otherNavigation'
docs.text: The location manager is being used to track movements for other types of vehicular navigation that are not automobile related.

docs.title: GeolocatorIosConfiguration
docs.text:
Bases: GeolocatorConfiguration

iOS specific settings.

docs.title: accuracy: GeolocatorPositionAccuracy = GeolocatorPositionAccuracy.BEST
docs.text: Defines the desired accuracy that should be used to determine the location data.

docs.title: activity_type: GeolocatorIosActivityType = GeolocatorIosActivityType.OTHER
docs.text: The location manager uses the information in this property as a cue to determine when location updates may be automatically paused.

docs.title: distance_filter: int = 0
docs.text:
The minimum distance (measured in meters) a device must move horizontally before an update event is generated.

Set to 0 when you want to be notified of all movements.

docs.title: show_background_location_indicator: bool = False
docs.text:
Flag to ask the Apple OS to show the background location indicator (iOS only) if app starts up and background and requests the users location.

For this setting to work and for the location to be retrieved the user must have granted "always" permissions for location retrieval.

docs.title: time_limit: ft.DurationValue = None
docs.text:
Specifies a timeout interval.

For no time limit, set to None.

docs.title: GeolocatorPermissionStatus
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Represent the possible location permissions.

docs.title: ALWAYS = 'always'
docs.text: Permission to access the device's location is allowed even when the app is running in the background.

docs.title: DENIED = 'denied'
docs.text:
Permission to access the device's location is denied.

The app should try to request permission using the [Geolocator.request_permission][flet_geolocator.Geolocator.request_permission] method.

docs.title: DENIED_FOREVER = 'deniedForever'
docs.text:
Permission to access the device's location is permanently denied.

When requesting permissions, the permission dialog will not be shown until the user updates the permission in the app settings.

docs.title: UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE = 'unableToDetermine'
docs.text:
Permission status cannot be determined.

This status is only returned by the [Geolocator.request_permission][flet_geolocator.Geolocator.request_permission] method on the web platform for browsers that did not implement the Permissions API. See: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Permissions_API

docs.title: WHILE_IN_USE = 'whileInUse'
docs.text: Permission to access the device's location is allowed only while the app is in use.

docs.title: GeolocatorPosition
docs.text: Detailed location information.

docs.title: accuracy: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The estimated horizontal accuracy of the position in meters.

The accuracy is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: altitude: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The altitude of the device in meters.

The altitude is not available on all devices. In these cases the returned value is 0.0.

docs.title: altitude_accuracy: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The estimated vertical accuracy of the position in meters.

The accuracy is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: floor: int | None = None
docs.text:
The floor specifies the floor of the building on which the device is located.

The floor property is only available on iOS and only when the information is available. In all other cases this value will be None.

docs.title: heading: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The heading in which the device is traveling in degrees.

The heading is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: heading_accuracy: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The estimated heading accuracy of the position in degrees.

The heading accuracy is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: latitude: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text: The latitude of this position in degrees normalized to the interval -90.0 to +90.0 (both inclusive).

docs.title: longitude: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text: The longitude of the position in degrees normalized to the interval -180 (exclusive) to +180 (inclusive).

docs.title: mocked: bool | None = None
docs.text:
Will be True on Android (starting from API level 18) when the location came from the mocked provider.

On iOS this value will always be False.

docs.title: speed: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The speed at which the device is traveling in meters per second over ground.

The speed is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: speed_accuracy: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text:
The estimated speed accuracy of this position, in meters per second.

The speed accuracy is not available on all devices. In these cases the value is 0.0.

docs.title: timestamp: datetime.datetime = None
docs.text: The time at which this position was determined.

docs.title: GeolocatorPositionAccuracy
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Represent the possible location accuracy values.

docs.title: BEST = 'best'
docs.text:
Location is accurate within a distance of ~0m on iOS and between 0m and 100m on Android.

On Android, corresponds to PRIORITY_HIGH_ACCURACY.

docs.title: BEST_FOR_NAVIGATION = 'bestForNavigation'
docs.text:
Location accuracy is optimized for navigation on iOS and matches the GeolocatorPositionAccuracy.BEST on Android.

On Android, corresponds to PRIORITY_HIGH_ACCURACY.

docs.title: HIGH = 'high'
docs.text:
Location is accurate within a distance of 10m on iOS and between 0m and 100m on Android.

On Android, corresponds to PRIORITY_HIGH_ACCURACY.

docs.title: LOW = 'low'
docs.text:
Location is accurate within a distance of 1000m on iOS and 500m on Android.

On Android, corresponds to PRIORITY_LOW_POWER.

docs.title: LOWEST = 'lowest'
docs.text:
Location is accurate within a distance of 3000m on iOS and 500m on Android.

On Android, corresponds to PRIORITY_PASSIVE.

docs.title: MEDIUM = 'medium'
docs.text:
Location is accurate within a distance of 100m on iOS and between 100m and 500m on Android.

On Android, corresponds to PRIORITY_BALANCED_POWER_ACCURACY.

docs.title: REDUCED = 'reduced'
docs.text:
Location accuracy is reduced for iOS 14+ devices. Matches GeolocatorPositionAccuracy.LOWEST on iOS 13 and below and all other platforms.

On Android, corresponds to PRIORITY_PASSIVE.

docs.title: GeolocatorPositionChangeEvent
docs.text: Bases: Event[EventControlType]

docs.title: position: GeolocatorPosition
docs.text: The current/new position of the device.

docs.title: GeolocatorWebConfiguration
docs.text:
Bases: GeolocatorConfiguration

Web specific settings.

docs.title: accuracy: GeolocatorPositionAccuracy = GeolocatorPositionAccuracy.BEST
docs.text: Defines the desired accuracy that should be used to determine the location data.

docs.title: distance_filter: int = 0
docs.text:
The minimum distance (measured in meters) a device must move horizontally before an update event is generated.

Set to 0 when you want to be notified of all movements.

docs.title: maximum_age: ft.DurationValue = field(default_factory=lambda: ft.Duration())
docs.text: A value indicating the maximum age of a possible cached position that is acceptable to return. If set to 0, it means that the device cannot use a cached position and must attempt to retrieve the real current position.

docs.title: time_limit: ft.DurationValue = None
docs.text:
Specifies a timeout interval.

For no time limit, set to None.
